messages.content:
(base) root@cyc:~/dev/hf-space/DataChef# git status
On branch main
Your branch is ahead of 'origin/main' by 2 commits.
  (use "git push" to publish your local commits)
Changes to be committed:
  (use "git restore --staged <file>..." to unstage)
        deleted:    logo.png
(base) root@cyc:~/dev/hf-space/DataChef# git add *
(base) root@cyc:~/dev/hf-space/DataChef# git commit -m "update ui"
[main adcf836] update ui
1 file changed, 0 insertions(+), 0 deletions(-)
delete mode 100644 logo.png
(base) root@cyc:~/dev/hf-space/DataChef# git push
Enumerating objects: 11, done.
Counting objects: 100% (11/11), done.
Delta compression using up to 4 threads
Compressing objects: 100% (9/9), done.
Writing objects: 100% (9/9), 1.11 MiB | 1.19 MiB/s, done.
Total 9 (delta 3), reused 0 (delta 0), pack-reused 0
remote: -------------------------------------------------------------------------
remote: Your push was rejected because it contains binary files.
remote: Please use https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/xet to store binary files.
remote: See also: https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/xet/using-xet-storage#git
remote:
remote: Offending files:
remote:   - logo.png (ref: refs/heads/main)
remote: -------------------------------------------------------------------------
To https://huggingface.co/spaces/yichengchen24/DataChef
! [remote rejected] main -> main (pre-receive hook declined)
error: failed to push some refs to 'https://huggingface.co/spaces/yichengchen24/DataChef'

Hugging Face rejected the push because your branch history contains a binary file (`logo.png`). Even though you *deleted* it in the latest commit, it still exists in earlier commits in the push range, so the server still sees it.

You have two options:

## Option A (recommended): enable HF Xet (stores binaries correctly) and push
From your repo root:

```bash
# install xet + enable it for this repo (or globally)
pip install -U "huggingface_hub[xet]"

# log in if needed
huggingface-cli login

# enable xet in this repo
huggingface-cli xet enable .

# now push again
git push
```

If you don’t want to use `pip`, you can install `xet` via other methods; the HF docs you saw in the error message cover it.

## Option B: remove the binary from your git history, then push
If you prefer not to use Xet, you must rewrite history to erase `logo.png` from all commits being pushed.

Using `git filter-repo` (cleanest):

```bash
pip install git-filter-repo

# rewrite history to remove the file everywhere
git filter-repo --path logo.png --invert-paths

# force-push rewritten history
git push --force-with-lease
```

### Notes
- After history rewrite, anyone else cloning/forking the repo will need to re-clone or reset.
- If you later add images/binaries again, you’ll hit the same restriction unless you use Xet.

If you tell me whether you want Xet or a history rewrite, I can tailor the exact commands to your situation (e.g., confirm whether `logo.png` appears in older commits and whether other binaries exist).

huggingface-cli xet enable .
usage: huggingface-cli <command> [<args>]
huggingface-cli: error: argument {download,upload,repo-files,env,login,whoami,logout,auth,repo,lfs-enable-largefiles,lfs-multipart-upload,scan-cache,delete-cache,tag,version,upload-large-folder}: invalid choice: 'xet' (choose from 'download', 'upload', 'repo-files', 'env', 'login', 'whoami', 'logout', 'auth', 'repo', 'lfs-enable-largefiles', 'lfs-multipart-upload', 'scan-cache', 'delete-cache', 'tag', 'version', 'upload-large-folder')

Your `huggingface-cli` is too old (or `huggingface_hub` was installed without the Xet extra), so it doesn’t have the `xet` subcommand.

## Fix: upgrade `huggingface_hub` with Xet support
```bash
pip install -U "huggingface_hub[xet]"
```

Verify:
```bash
huggingface-cli version
huggingface-cli xet --help
```

Then enable Xet in the repo:
```bash
cd /root/dev/hf-space/DataChef
huggingface-cli xet enable .
git push
```

## If you can’t/ don’t want to upgrade: rewrite history to remove `logo.png`
This avoids needing Xet, because HF will no longer see the binary in any commit you’re pushing:

```bash
cd /root/dev/hf-space/DataChef
pip install -U git-filter-repo
git filter-repo --path logo.png --invert-paths
git push --force-with-lease
```

## Quick check (optional): confirm it exists in history
```bash
git log --name-only -- logo.png
```

If you paste the output of `pip show huggingface_hub` and `huggingface-cli version`, I can tell you exactly what version you’re on and which path will be smoothest.
我不想上传这个 png 了，可以清空 git，重新传吗

可以。思路是：把本地仓库历史全部丢掉，重新做成“只有当前文件的一次提交”，然后强推到 Hugging Face。这样历史里就不会再包含那个 `logo.png`。

### 方案：重置为全新 Git 历史并强推
在仓库目录执行：

```bash
cd /root/dev/hf-space/DataChef

# 1) 新建一个干净分支（不带任何历史）
git checkout --orphan clean

# 2) 清空暂存区/索引里原来的跟踪（保留工作区文件）
git rm -r --cached .

# 3) 把当前工作区内容重新加入（确保 logo.png 不在目录里）
git add -A

# 4) 提交一次“全新初始提交”
git commit -m "clean history"

# 5) 用这个干净分支覆盖 main
git branch -M main

# 6) 强推覆盖远端
git push --force-with-lease origin main
```

### 注意
- 这会彻底重写远端 `main` 的历史；如果还有其他人协作，他们需要重新拉取/重克隆。
- 推之前确认 `logo.png` 真的不在当前目录里（否则会再次被拒）：
  ```bash
  ls -la | grep logo.png
  ```

如果你希望“远端也完全清空（删掉 Space 再重建）”，也可以走删除仓库/Space 再重新 push 的路，但上面这种一般就够了。
